name: Raspberry Chocolate Swirl Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
-CRUST:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Combine the vanilla wafer crumbs, cocoa powder, and powdered sugar in a bowl. Stir in the melted butter until well combined.
3. Press the mixture onto the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
4. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until lightly golden. Remove from oven and let cool completely.
-FILLING:
1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese with 3/4 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon vanilla until smooth.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. In a small bowl, stir together the cocoa powder and remaining 1/4 cup sugar. Mix in the vegetable oil and remaining 1/2 teaspoon vanilla.
5. Gradually add the cheese mixture to the batter, alternating with the red raspberry spread. Blend in thoroughly.
6. Fold in the flour until well incorporated.
7. Pour half of the raspberry mixture into the pan. Dollop about half of the chocolate mixture on top.
8. Repeat with the remaining mixture, finishing with dollops of chocolate on top.
9. Gently swirl the layers with a knife to create an even swirl pattern.
10. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 250 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 55 minutes until set.
End of recipe instructions.
